parsed_review.summary: CP00-199 (RP06 DMS core type-and-shape + service descriptor, risk class B, 40 units RP06.P01.M02.S09-RP06.P01.M04.S06) is a descriptor-only/synthetic-only pack and its scoped claims are supported by the implementation, tests, contract snapshot, and review scaffolding. The pack does NOT overclaim: manifest.json holds production_ready:false / status:in_progress, and production_ready_flag is the descriptive label "dms_core_type_shape_service_descriptor_verified" rather than a production/enterprise-trust assertion. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced redundantly: registry no-write attestations (writes_product_state, evaluates_runtime_permission, writes_audit_event, executes_object_storage_read, executes_ocr, executes_search_indexing, implements_loop_engine, promotes_claude_to_final_approval, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation) are all pinned false, and serialization shape excludes raw_payload/document_bytes/extracted_text/permission_decision_detail/audit_event_body; the synthetic fixture uses only synthetic IDs (tenant_rp06_synthetic), zero byte_size, and sha256 zero-fill. validators.js validateDmsCoreCp199SerializedDescriptor independently enforces *_included===false on every record. The committed contract (contracts/dms-core-contract.json) is snapshot-tested against the live foundation descriptor via assert.deepEqual in model.test.js, and scripts/validate-rp06-dms-core-contract.mjs pins program RP06/upstream CP00-197/current_pack CP00-199. Authority boundary is respected: claude_packet read_only:true with allowed_tools Read/Grep/Glob, manifest claude_not_final_approver:true and enterprise_trust_not_claimed_from_local_validation:true; nothing promotes Claude to final approver or claims enterprise trust from local validation. Hermes/Claude (gates H06/C06) appear only as deterministic review-packet/receipt descriptors, not embedded in product runtime. CP178+ review-evidence hardening is satisfied for this step: auth_preflight passed (exit 0, 1432-byte valid-JSON stdout), prompt is 2240 bytes (under 25600 limit), forbidden_tools_present false, summary-only review prohibited and not used. Two P3 (non-blocking, informational) findings only: a dead serialization-guard branch and the expected absence of downstream pack artifacts that are produced in later pipeline steps. No P0/P1/P2. This read-only review does not constitute final approval; human final approval remains required for any enterprise-trust claim.

findings.title: Dead serialization-guard branch in CP199 type-shape validator
findings.details: In validateDmsCoreCp199TypeShape, the loop over DMS_CORE_CP199_REQUIREMENTS.required_serialization_guards guards on `!DMS_CORE_SERIALIZATION_SHAPE.fields.includes("no_write_attestation") && guard === "no_write_attestation"`. The serialization-shape `fields` list enumerates leakable payload field names (raw_payload, document_bytes, extracted_text, permission_decision_detail, audit_event_body) and does not contain the literal "no_write_attestation"; meanwhile required_serialization_guards is not composed of that literal in a way that makes `guard === "no_write_attestation"` reachable, so the push of "CP00-199 serialization missing no_write_attestation" is effectively unreachable. The intended invariant (descriptor never serializes write/leak payloads) is already enforced independently and correctly by validateDmsCoreCp199SerializedDescriptor (lines 347-349), which asserts raw_payload_included/document_bytes_included/extracted_text_included/permission_decision_detail_included/audit_event_body_included === false on every record, plus the explicit no-write attestation checks at lines 356-366. So this is redundant dead code, not a coverage gap.
findings.recommendation: Either remove the dead loop (lines 351-355) or rewrite it so the guard literal is actually compared against required_serialization_guards membership, to avoid future readers assuming a check exists where it does not. Non-blocking; the no-leak invariant is already enforced elsewhere.
findings.post_review_disposition: Logged as P3 cleanup; does not block CP00-199 pack or goal closeout because the serialization no-leak invariant is independently enforced and tested.

findings.title: Downstream pack artifacts not yet present (expected at this review step)
findings.details: manifest.required_pack_artifacts lists five deliverables: manifest.json, command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json. Only manifest.json currently exists. This is expected: the current stage is the read-only Claude raw-capture step (step 3 of the hardened sequence baseline -> readiness -> read_only_claude_raw_capture -> normalize -> receipt_validate -> adjudication -> construction_inspection -> final_closeout_validation). The remaining four artifacts are produced by later pipeline stages (normalize/receipt, adjudication, construction inspection) and their absence now is not evidence of an incomplete pack. pack_level_claude_review.status is pending_hardened_review and the manifest carries production_ready:false / status:in_progress consistent with this stage.
findings.recommendation: No action for this reviewer. Confirm during final_closeout_validation that all five required_pack_artifacts exist and that claude-review-result.json embeds this verdict before flipping pack production_ready. Flagged only so the absence is not mistaken for a regression.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational; non-blocking. Final presence/validation of these artifacts is owned by downstream pipeline stages and human final approval, not by this read-only review.
